title: Rhino Scene-Graph Artifact
description: Externalized world model owned by the rhino-scene-state skill. It is the canonical, re-read-every-step record of what actually exists in the live Rhino document: a part_id -> GUID ledger plus a bbox/dimension ledger, and the realized spatial relations between parts. It is the realized counterpart to the build-plan IR (shared/build-plan.schema.json): the IR declares intent, the scene-graph records the baked truth captured at bake time. Producers append GUIDs out-of-band here; the reconcile loop diffs this artifact's nodes against a live document summary to flag MISSING / EXTRA / MIS-SIZED parts and post-boolean count/volume mismatches (corrections C1 and C2).

object.description: Plain-language name of the modeled object, copied from the build-plan IR 'object' field for provenance and verify questions.

units.description: Document unit system, copied from the IR. Every bbox/dims/origin number in this artifact is in these units. Must match sc.doc.ModelUnitSystem at bake time.

tolerance.description: Absolute model tolerance in 'units', copied from the IR / read live off sc.doc.ModelAbsoluteTolerance. Used as the default bbox/dimension match tolerance during reconcile unless overridden per-node.

world_frame.description: Named base construction plane all node local frames are relative to. Default WorldXY.

revision.description: Monotonically increasing artifact revision, bumped after every mutation cycle so re-reads can detect staleness.

last_op.description: Short label of the most recent mutation that produced this revision, e.g. 'boolean_union:seat+legs' or 'bake:leg[2]'. Aids the reconcile report and debugging.

nodes.description: Realized parts. Each node mirrors one build-plan IR part (by part_id) and records its baked GUID, world-axis-aligned bounding box, and key dimensions captured from the live document.

edges.description: Realized spatial/logical relations between nodes, mirroring the IR relation set. Each edge names a relation type and its two endpoint part_ids.

checkpoints.description: Per-stage build checkpoints (conventions §12). One entry per stage that has been baked + reconciled, recording its pass/fail status and the revision at which it last passed. Lets a staged build resume after a crash, lets a re-emit of one stage skip already-passing stages, and (with the .3dm Save the modeling skill triggers at each checkpoint) is the persisted ledger that survives a rollback (E11).

stage.description: Stage id, matching node.stage and the IR stages[].id.

status.description: passed = this stage baked and reconciled clean; failed = reconcile flagged a defect not yet repaired; pending = declared but not yet built.

revision.description: scene-graph revision at which this stage last reached 'status'. The .3dm was saved at this revision (E11).

part_ids.description: Part ids this checkpoint covers (the stage's parts), so a re-emit knows exactly what to purge + rebuild.

point3d.description: [x, y, z] in document 'units' against world_frame.

bbox.description: World-axis-aligned bounding box captured from the baked geometry via Rhino.Geometry.BoundingBox (GetBoundingBox(True)). min and max are opposite corners in 'units'.

frame.description: The local construction frame the part was authored on, copied from the IR part.frame. Lets a re-read reconstruct where the part lives without re-querying geometry.

plane.description: Named target plane (WorldXY, WorldYZ, WorldZX/WorldXZ, or a custom frame name) the part was relocated onto.

x_axis.description: Optional explicit plane X axis vector.

y_axis.description: Optional explicit plane Y axis vector.

node.description: One realized part. MUST carry at least one stable handle: a 'guid' (the canonical Rhino reference) OR a 'part_id' (the UserString fallback resolver, correction C1). In steady state both are present; immediately after a mutator that has not yet returned its GUID, part_id alone is permitted and the reconcile loop backfills the guid.

part_id.description: Stable part identifier, identical to the IR part.id and to the baked object's UserString 'part_id'. The fallback resolver when a GUID is lost (e.g. after a boolean consumes inputs).

guid.description: The Rhino System.Guid returned by AddBrep/AddCurve/AddSurface at bake time, as a string. The canonical object handle (correction C1). May be the unset GUID string only transiently before backfill.

name.description: Rhino object Name (human-readable in the object table), set equal to part_id at bake time.

layer.description: Target Rhino layer name the object lives on, mirroring the IR part.layer.

stage.description: Build stage that baked this node, mirroring the IR part.stage / stages[].id and the baked object's UserString 'stage' (conventions §12). Lets reconcile run SCOPED to a single stage (--stage) so re-emitting one stage is checked against only that stage's expected nodes, not the whole model.

primitive.description: How the geometry was produced: a primitive type ('box','cylinder','sphere','cone','plane','interpolated_curve') or an operation result ('loft','sweep1','revolve','extrude','shell','boolean'). Free-form to allow operation results.

frame.description: Local construction frame the part was authored on (copied from the IR).

bbox.description: World-axis-aligned bounding box captured from the live baked geometry.

dims.description: Key realized dimensions in 'units', e.g. {x,y,z} for a box, {radius,height} for a cylinder, or {width,depth,height} derived from the bbox. Used by reconcile MIS-SIZED checks.

volume.description: Realized solid volume in 'units^3' from Rhino.Geometry.VolumeMassProperties.Compute, when the node is a closed solid. Used by the post-boolean total-volume check (correction C2). Omitted for open curves/surfaces.

is_solid.description: Whether the baked Brep is a closed solid (Brep.IsSolid). Open curves/surfaces are false.

expected_solid_count.description: For boolean/operation results: how many disjoint solids the result SHOULD contain per the IR boolean_plan. The reconcile loop compares this to the realized count to catch partial boolean failure (correction C2).

provenance.description: Why this part exists / where its shape came from, copied from the IR part.provenance.

color.description: Optional vision color assigned for a colored-part capture (correction C4), e.g. 'red' or '255,0,0'. A render concern, not an identity concern.

edge.description: A realized relation between two nodes, mirroring an IR relation. For 'interpenetrate' edges (union joins) the realized overlap depth is recorded in 'penetration' for the correction-C3 check.

type.description: Relation kind. 'child_of' models an operation result owning its consumed inputs; 'interpenetrate' models a union join overlap.

from.description: part_id of the node this relation is anchored on.

to.description: part_id of the other node, or a symmetry/frame name for 'symmetric_about'.

penetration.description: Realized overlap depth in 'units' (target 0.5-2mm) for 'interpenetrate' edges feeding a boolean union (correction C3). Required for interpenetrate edges.
